rulings.Case Title: LARRY SUBLETT VS. KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Limbach Facility Services, Llcs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 19, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Limbach Facility Services, LLC's motion to quash service of summons is granted. Plaintiff failed to meet his burden of demonstrating that this Court has personal jurisdiction over defendant because he failed to demonstrate that defendant is liable for the Western Air & Refrigeration entity that plaintiff alleges exposed him to asbestos. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
